speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Thus have you heard our cause and known our means,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And, my most noble friends, I pray you all

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Speak plainly your opinions of our hopes.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And first, Lord Marshal, what say you to it?

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I well allow the occasion of our arms,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: But gladly would be better satisfied

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: How in our means we should advance ourselves

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To look with forehead bold and big enough

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Upon the power and puissance of the King.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Our present musters grow upon the file

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To five-and-twenty thousand men of choice,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And our supplies live largely in the hope

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Of great Northumberland, whose bosom burns

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With an incensèd fire of injuries.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: The question, then, Lord Hastings, standeth thus:

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Whether our present five-and-twenty thousand

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: May hold up head without Northumberland.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With him we may.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Yea, marry, there’s the point.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: But if without him we be thought too feeble,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: My judgment is we should not step too far

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Till we had his assistance by the hand.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: For in a theme so bloody-faced as this,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Conjecture, expectation, and surmise

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Of aids incertain should not be admitted.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: ’Tis very true, Lord Bardolph, for indeed

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: It was young Hotspur’s cause at Shrewsbury.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: It was, my lord; who lined himself with hope,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Eating the air and promise of supply,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Flatt’ring himself in project of a power

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Much smaller than the smallest of his thoughts,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And so, with great imagination

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Proper to madmen, led his powers to death

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And, winking, leapt into destruction.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But, by your leave, it never yet did hurt

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To lay down likelihoods and forms of hope.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Yes, if this present quality of war —

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Indeed the instant action, a cause on foot—

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Lives so in hope, as in an early spring

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: We see th’ appearing buds, which to prove fruit

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Hope gives not so much warrant as despair

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: That frosts will bite them. When we mean to build,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: We first survey the plot, then draw the model,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And when we see the figure of the house,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Then must we rate the cost of the erection,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Which if we find outweighs ability,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: What do we then but draw anew the model

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: In fewer offices, or at least desist

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: To build at all? Much more in this great work,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Which is almost to pluck a kingdom down

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And set another up, should we survey

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: The plot of situation and the model,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Consent upon a sure foundation,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Question surveyors, know our own estate,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: How able such a work to undergo,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: To weigh against his opposite. Or else

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: We fortify in paper and in figures,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Using the names of men instead of men,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Like one that draws the model of an house

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Beyond his power to build it, who, half through,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Gives o’er and leaves his part-created cost

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: A naked subject to the weeping clouds

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And waste for churlish winter’s tyranny.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Grant that our hopes, yet likely of fair birth,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Should be stillborn and that we now possessed

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The utmost man of expectation,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I think we are a body strong enough,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Even as we are, to equal with the King.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: What, is the King but five-and-twenty thousand?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To us no more, nay, not so much, Lord Bardolph,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: For his divisions, as the times do brawl,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Are in three heads: one power against the French,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And one against Glendower; perforce a third

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Must take up us. So is the unfirm king

speaker: HASTINGS
text: In three divided, and his coffers sound

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With hollow poverty and emptiness.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That he should draw his several strengths together

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And come against us in full puissance

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Need not to be dreaded.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: If he should do so,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: He leaves his back unarmed, the French and Welsh

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Baying him at the heels. Never fear that.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Who is it like should lead his forces hither?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The Duke of Lancaster and Westmoreland;

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Against the Welsh, himself and Harry Monmouth;

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But who is substituted against the French

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I have no certain notice.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Let us on,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And publish the occasion of our arms.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The commonwealth is sick of their own choice.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Their over-greedy love hath surfeited.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: An habitation giddy and unsure

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Hath he that buildeth on the vulgar heart.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: O thou fond many, with what loud applause

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Didst thou beat heaven with blessing Bolingbroke

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Before he was what thou wouldst have him be.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And being now trimmed in thine own desires,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Thou, beastly feeder, art so full of him

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That thou provok’st thyself to cast him up.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: So, so, thou common dog, didst thou disgorge

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Thy glutton bosom of the royal Richard,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And now thou wouldst eat thy dead vomit up

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And howl’st to find it. What trust is in these times?

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: They that, when Richard lived, would have him die

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Are now become enamored on his grave.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Thou, that threw’st dust upon his goodly head

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: When through proud London he came sighing on

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: After th’ admirèd heels of Bolingbroke,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Criest now “O earth, yield us that king again,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And take thou this!” O thoughts of men accursed!

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Past and to come seems best; things present, worst.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Shall we go draw our numbers and set on?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: We are time’s subjects, and time bids begone.
